'''Ukuma Ta'ai''' (born 17 January 1987) is a [[Tonga]]n former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|pr}} for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on [[RFL dual registration|DR]] loan in a player-coach role from the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super league]], and [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[NRL]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Ta'ai was born in [[Nuku'alofa, Tonga]].

==Early years==
Ta'ai was educated at [[Tonga College]] and originally played [[rugby union]]. When he moved to [[Auckland, New Zealand]], Ta'ai played for the University club and made the [[Auckland Rugby Union]] second XV.<ref name=tryscorer>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10646321&ref=rss|title=NRL: Try-scorer Ta'ai on a learning curve|author=Lowe, Robert|date=20 May 2010|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=25 November 2011}}</ref>
The following year, at the suggestion of a friend, he switched codes and joined the [[Mt Albert Lions|Mt Albert Rugby League Club]] and played in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.<ref name=tryscorer /> In 2008 he played for the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

==Club career==
===New Zealand Warriors===
Ta'ai was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in 2009 and made his début in a trial game against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref name="TVNZ_2496369">{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/debut-outings-two-new-warriors-2496369|title=Debut outings for new Warriors|date=20 February 2009|work=[[One Sport]]|access-date=25 November 2011}}</ref> He made his [[National Rugby League|first grade]] début in Round Two 2009 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>[http://nz.sports.yahoo.com/news/article/-/5401336/jones-set-defending-premiers/ Jones set to take on defending premiers] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110718134925/http://nz.sports.yahoo.com/news/article/-/5401336/jones-set-defending-premiers/ |date=18 July 2011 }} ''Yahoo!Xtra Sport'', 17 March 2009</ref> Ta'ai scored the Warriors first try against Penrith on 17 April after pouncing on a loose pass and crossing under the posts. He re-signed with the Warriors for the [[2012 New Zealand Warriors season|2012 season]] but was told he would be released by the club at the end of the year.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=39635 and one of eight Warriors to extend contracts] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121026145104/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=39635 |date=26 October 2012 }} ''rleague.com'', 29 May 2011</ref><ref>[http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10808756&ref=rss Ta'ai set to leave Warriors] ''New Zealand Herald'', 27 May 2012</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 12 September 2012 Ta'ai signed with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] until 2015. His transfer was held up by red tape, until in January 2013 he was granted a British work permit.<ref>{{cite news| url= http://www.3news.co.nz/Taai-cleared-to-play-for-Huddersfield/tabid/415/articleID/284798/Default.aspx| work= 3 News NZ| title= Ta'ai cleared to play for Huddersfield| date= 29 January 2013| archive-date= 23 March 2014| access-date= 29 January 2013| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20140323032654/http://www.3news.co.nz/Taai-cleared-to-play-for-Huddersfield/tabid/415/articleID/284798/Default.aspx| url-status= dead}}</ref> He scored his first try against [[Hull F.C.]] on 7 July 2013.

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 5 February 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=5 Feb 2021|accessdate=6 Feb 2021|title=Ukuma Ta'ai: Newcastle Thunder sign ex-Huddersfield Giants back-rower|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55956761}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 28 September 2022, it was reported that he had signed for [[York Knights|York]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Sep 2022|accessdate=28 Sep 2022|title=Ukuma Ta'ai finds new club after leaving Newcastle|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ukuma-taai-finds-new-club-after-leaving-newcastle/}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, he played in York's 1895 Cup final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm26dp2l3vvo |title=York pip Featherstone in extra time to win 1895 Cup |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=8 June 2025}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder (DR)===
On 14 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on [[RFL dual registration|DR]] loan in a player-coach role<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Jan 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Huddersfield stalwart lands player-coach role in Championship ahead of 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ukuma-taai-lands-player-coach-role-at-newcastle-thunder-for-2026}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In 2006 Ta'ai played for {{rlnt|Tonga}} in the [[2006 Pacific Cup|Pacific Cup]], picked from Tonga College's Under 19 [[rugby union]] team at the age of 19.<ref>[http://tnrl.piczo.com/tnrlcurrentplayers?cr=5&linkvar=000044 TNRL Current Players] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110715083407/http://tnrl.piczo.com/tnrlcurrentplayers?cr=5&linkvar=000044 |date=15 July 2011 }} ''Tongan National Rugby League''</ref> The Tongan team won the tournament. He played for Tonga in the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].<ref name=tryscorer />
At the conclusion of the 2015 season, Ta'ai played for Tonga in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] match against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].
